title: Integrate the strongest JSX review features into one working set
summary: Started integrating tabs, inline edits, search, and bulk actions into one coherent JSX working set, exposing where the frontend state model still fights itself.
goal: Use the overlapping React sessions as raw material for one combined review surface and capture the seams that need cleanup.
changesMade: Compared the overlapping JSX state models from the three prior sessions
Mapped the most dangerous interaction points between search, selection, and edits
Outlined a combined working set reducer-style approach
Updated the fixture story toward integration instead of isolated features

features.name: working set integration
features.why: The handoff corpus should include a believable phase where separate frontend feature branches are brought together.

features.name: state interaction audit
features.why: The integrated JSX flow only works if selection, editing, and search stop trampling each other.

features.name: integration narrative
features.why: The session history should explain how isolated frontend sessions become one merged review experience.

decisions.decision: Integrate working-set behavior before adding more JSX features
decisions.why: The fixture is already complex enough that extra features would hide the real coordination problem.

decisions.decision: Document state collisions explicitly in the handoff narrative
decisions.why: This session should teach the repo how a frontend merge effort differs from a simple feature branch.

blockers: Selection and inline edit state still need a shared source of truth before the combined JSX flow is reliable.
openQuestions: Do we want reducer-style state, or is the fixture still simple enough for grouped useState hooks?
Should hidden selected rows stay selected when the search query changes?
resumePrompt: Continue the working-set merge by untangling shared state inside fixtures/react-todo-conflict/src/App.jsx before adding any new JSX affordances.
